---
title: "Script to work with Satellite Embeddings dataset bands derived from GEE"
subtitle: "Read and manipulation data, calculate indices"
author: "Alicia Valdés"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_notebook: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
```

# Load libraries

```{r}
library(signal)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lubridate)
library(dtplyr)
library(sf)
library(knitr)
library(mgcv)
library(future)
library(furrr)
library(progressr)
library(pracma)
```

# Load functions

```{r}
knitr::purl("functions.Rmd", output = "functions.R")
source("functions.R")
```


# Supress package messages

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(mgcv)
  library(nlme)
})
```

# Read files with band data

I got these files using the GEE code prepared by Bea.

These files contain all observations in the ReSurvey database from 2016 onward. In order to avoid computation problems in GEE, biogeographical units that contain more than 4500 points have been subdivided in ArcGIS.

```{r}
# Set the folder path
folder_path <- "C:/Data/MOTIVATE/MOTIVATE_RS_data/Satellite_Embeddings"

# List CSV files
csv_files <- list.files(folder_path, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)

# Read one file to get column names
sample_file <- csv_files[1]
sample_data <- read_csv(sample_file, n_max = 0)

# Identify columns starting with "EUNIS"
eunis_cols <- names(sample_data)[startsWith(names(sample_data), "EUNIS")]

# Create a spec list
spec_list <- setNames(rep(list(col_character()), length(eunis_cols)), eunis_cols)

# Add fixed columns
spec_list$RSrvypl <- col_character()
spec_list$RSrvyst <- col_character()
spec_list$RSrvyob <- col_character()

# Create final column spec
custom_col_types <- do.call(cols, spec_list)

# Read and process each file
data_list <- lapply(csv_files, function(file) {
  info <- extract_info(basename(file)) # Use only the filename
  
  # Read the file
  df <- read_csv(file, col_types = custom_col_types) %>%
    mutate(biogeo = info$biogeo, unit = info$unit)
  
  return(df)
  })

# Combine all data
data_RS_SatEmb_bands <- bind_rows(data_list) %>%
  rename(PlotObservationID = PltObID)

# View the resulting tibble
print(data_RS_SatEmb_bands)

# Counts per biogeo and unit
print(data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>% count(biogeo, unit), n = 100)
```

# Remove unneded cols

```{r}
data_RS_SatEmb_bands <- data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>%
  select(-starts_with("X"), -UNIT)
```

# Read file with band data for points outside polygons

Some points from the ReSurvey database fall outside the bioregion polygons. These points were manually assigned to the closest biogeo and unit in ArcGIS (those far out were assigned biogeo = OUT and unit = OUT). Data for these points has been extracted with a different GEE script.

```{r}
data_RS_SatEmb_bands_out <- read_csv(
  "C:/Data/MOTIVATE/MOTIVATE_RS_data/Satellite_Embeddings_out/AllPoints_SatelliteEmbedding_Annual.csv",
  col_types = custom_col_types) %>%
  select(-AREA, -Join_Count, -TARGET_FID, -code, -name) %>%
  rename(biogeo = BIOGEO, unit = UNIT, `FEVA(Y/` = `FEVA_Y_`, `Mn(y/n)` = `Mn_y_n_`,
         PlotObservationID = PltObID)
```

Check that column names are the same:

```{r}
# Columns in data_RS_SatEmb_bands but not in data_RS_SatEmb_bands_out
setdiff(names(data_RS_SatEmb_bands), names(data_RS_SatEmb_bands_out))

# Columns in data_RS_SatEmb_bands_out but not in data_RS_SatEmb_bands
setdiff(names(data_RS_SatEmb_bands_out), names(data_RS_SatEmb_bands))
```

# Join all data

```{r}
data_RS_SatEmb_bands <- bind_rows(data_RS_SatEmb_bands,
                                  data_RS_SatEmb_bands_out)
```

# Solve problem with duplicate rows

```{r}
data_RS_SatEmb_bands <- data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>%
  select(-`system:index`, -image_id, -time_utc, -timestamp, -.geo)
```

```{r}
# Find duplicated ids
dupl_ids <- data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>%
  group_by(PlotObservationID) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n() > 1) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
dupl_ids
```

Group the data by PlotObservationID, and for duplicate ids, average the values of the bands (and keep the first value for other cols).

```{r}
data_RS_SatEmb_bands <- data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>%
  group_by(PlotObservationID) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("A"), mean, na.rm = TRUE),
            across(!starts_with("A"), first), .groups = "drop")
```

# Add canopy height data

Read the data:

```{r}
data_RS_CH <- read_csv(
  "C:/Data/MOTIVATE/MOTIVATE_RS_data/Canopy_Height_1m/CanopyHeight_1m.csv")
```

Join:

```{r}
final_RS_data <- data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>%
  mutate(PlotObservationID = factor(PlotObservationID)) %>%
  left_join(data_RS_CH %>% 
              dplyr::select(PltObID, canopy_height) %>%
              rename(PlotObservationID = PltObID) %>%
              mutate(PlotObservationID = factor(PlotObservationID)))
```

# See mismatches

```{r}
read_delim(
  # Read data from ArcGIS Pro spatial join points - N2K bioregions
  here("data", "clean", "ReSurvey_QRST_within_outside_polygons.csv"), 
  delim = ";") %>%
  dplyr::filter(year >= 2017) %>%
  count(BIOGEO, UNIT) %>%
  select(UNIT, n) %>%
  rename(unit = UNIT, n_GIS = n) %>%
  left_join(
    data_RS_SatEmb_bands %>% count(biogeo, unit) %>%
      mutate(unit = ifelse(is.na(unit), biogeo, paste0(biogeo, unit))) %>%
      select(unit, n) %>%
      rename(n_R = n)
  ) %>%
  mutate(diff = n_GIS - n_R) %>%
  print(n = 100)
```

n_GIS > n_R: should be because there is no Satellite Embeding data for some reason.
n_GIS < n_R: not sure why, different way of assessing if points are within polygons in ArcGIS Pro and GEE? Maybe it has to do with using filterBounds in GEE? I can maybe look into this later, but probably it will not matter much.

# MAYBE TBD LATER: investigate mismatches

# Save to clean data

```{r}
write_tsv(final_RS_data,
          here("data", "clean","final_RS_data_SatEmb_20251010.csv"))
```

# Session info

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
